summary: I was recruited in the Philippines back in 1962. Joining the service seemed like a great opportunity to come to the United States. At that time, the Navy was the only branch recruiting Filipino citizens, so that's why I chose it. My early days in service started there before I went on to other assignments.

I served during the Vietnam War and was assigned to Cameron Bay in Vietnam. Although we were in a combat area, my unit was mostly in support roles and we never engaged directly in firefights. I remember nights when mortar fire came in, and we had to take cover quickly. I was awarded the Vietnam Service Medal for my time there.

Cameron Bay in Vietnam was where I was stationed during the war. We rotated there every two weeks from our home port. It was a tense place, especially at night when we sometimes came under mortar fire. Despite the danger, my unit didn’t suffer any casualties while we were there.

I joined the United States Navy in 1962 because it was the only branch recruiting in the Philippines at the time. My role was as a ship serviceman, and I traveled extensively, including to places like Iceland, San Diego, Florida, and the Great Lakes. Serving in the Navy gave me the chance to see the world and provided good benefits, including medals and educational support after service.

I remember Bob Hope coming aboard ships to entertain us during the war. He brought his showgirls and put on performances that lifted our spirits while we were at sea. His visits were special moments of relief and entertainment during tough times.

During my Navy service, I was stationed in Iceland at one point. It was part of the many places I traveled to while serving, along with San Diego, Florida, and the Great Lakes. Being stationed there was part of the broader experience of moving every couple of years with ship assignments.

San Diego was one of my home ports and where I was aboard the USS Meyerkord when I ended my service. The United States Naval Station there was a key place for resupply and rotation during my Navy career. I also spent time on leave in San Diego, enjoying moments with my family.

Florida was one of the places I traveled to during my Navy service. Along with other locations like San Diego and the Great Lakes, Florida was part of the many assignments and travels that came with being in the Navy. These travels helped me see different parts of the world.

I was stationed at the Great Lakes Naval Training Station during my time in the Navy. It was one of the key training and assignment locations before moving on to ship duty. The experience there was part of my overall Navy journey that included many travels and rotations.

I was aboard the USS Meyerkord in San Diego when my service ended. It was a significant moment marked by a small ceremony where they piped me out and announced my departure. Serving on the Meyerkord was part of my final days in the Navy before transitioning back to civilian life.

entity: United States Naval Station San Diego
summary: The United States Naval Station in San Diego was an important base during my Navy career. It served as a home port and a place for resupply and rotation. I was there when I ended my service aboard the USS Meyerkord, marking the close of my military journey.
cues: United States Naval Station San Diego

summary: I was awarded four Good Conduct Medals during my Navy service. These medals recognize having no bad records for four consecutive years, which I was proud to maintain. It was a reflection of my discipline and commitment throughout my military career.

I received the Vietnam Service Medal automatically once I was assigned to Vietnam. It marked my participation in the conflict, even though my unit was mostly in support roles. The medal symbolizes my time serving in a combat zone during the Vietnam War.

I was also awarded the National Defense Service Medal during my time in the Navy. This medal recognized my service during a period of national conflict. It was one of several honors that acknowledged my commitment to the military.
